name: Example Python Module
description: Reference ASLM Python module: Flask WebView UI, typed settings (getExec/setExec), downloads bridge (stdio JSON), host theme/locale snapshots, module interop HTTP client, and host-managed python-runtime venv.
author: NEXTGGTECH

firstRun.name: Initialize settings.json
firstRun.description: Creates Settings/settings.json from manifest defaults (first install or after update).

run.name: Flask WebView server
run.description: Binds to ui-port and serves App/ for the ASLM module page (ASLM_UI_PORT).

settings.name: HTTP listen port
settings.description: TCP port for main.py runserver. Type port: ASLM reserves the port, injects ASLM_UI_PORT, and passes the numeric value to setExec.

settings.name: Dashboard banner text
settings.description: Plain string stored in settings.json and exported as ASLM_GREETING when the module runs. Use for module title or build label in your UI.
settings.default: Example Python Module — ASLM reference UI

settings.name: Expose runtime diagnostics
settings.description: Type bool: when true, the dashboard shows ASLM_* environment variables, HTTP exchange logs, and raw JSON snapshots (intended for module authors).

settings.name: Auto-refresh interval (ms)
settings.description: Type int: base period in milliseconds for UI polling (e.g. module interop registry refresh). The dashboard default timer uses 10000 ms when this value is 10000.

settings.name: Log sampling rate
settings.description: Type number (float): fraction between 0 and 1 for sampling verbose logs in custom bridge/interop code (1.0 = log every event, 0.25 = one in four).

settings.name: Outbound API token
settings.description: Type password: secret for Authorization headers in sample HTTP clients. Masked in ASLM settings UI; redacted in GET /api/settings (shows *** when set).

settings.name: Python log level
settings.description: Type select: controls logging.basicConfig level in main.py subcommands. Allowed: basic (INFO), debug (DEBUG), trace (DEBUG + verbose handlers).

settings.name: python-runtime engine
settings.description: Type engine: read-only mirror of whether ASLM installed the python-runtime engine for this module. false if the engine package is missing.

settings.name: Python executable (engine path)
settings.description: Type path: absolute path to python.exe in the host venv (Engines/Python/venv-<module-id>/). ASLM manages the value; override via Custom Value only when debugging.

settings.name: Engine data directory
settings.description: Type data: writable per-engine data root (Data/python-runtime/ under the module). Use for caches, SQLite DBs, or bridge download targets (targetRef).

settings.name: Engine models directory
settings.description: Type models: optional Models/python-runtime/ path for ML weights or large artifacts. ASLM creates the directory; same contract as data but semantically for models.

settings.name: Host theme snapshot
settings.description: Type theme: host-only. ASLM writes JSON (appearance, theme, colors) and calls apply_aslm_host_theme --file {path}. Persist to Settings/host_theme.json and map colors.* to CSS.

settings.name: Host locale snapshot
settings.description: Type locale: host-only. ASLM writes JSON (language, displayName) and calls apply_aslm_locale --file {path}. Load Settings/host_locale.json to localize module UI.

categories.title: Python reference artifacts
categories.description: Sample entries that document downloads bridge request/response fields. resolve_install returns step templates (download_file, extract_zip, python_package) without downloading in this reference module.

example_data.description: Install root: <module>/Data/example-downloads/ — used as targetRef in installManifest/uninstallManifest from this bridge.
